name: Workstream Analysis
description: Comprehensive workstream health analysis that detects breaking issues, performance problems, anti-patterns, and resource waste. Traces root causes and suggests actionable improvements.

mode.description: Analysis mode: 'single' for one workstream deep-dive, 'cross' for pattern analysis across multiple workstreams

workstreamId.description: For 'single' mode: The workstream ID to analyze (0x-prefixed hex, or 'current')

rootJobName.description: For 'cross' mode: Root job name pattern to find related workstreams (e.g., 'blog-automation')

maxWorkstreams.description: For 'cross' mode: Maximum number of workstreams to analyze

focusArea.description: Optional: natural language guidance for analysis focus

invariants.assessment: Calculate (jobs inspected / total jobs in scope) × 100. Use inspect_workstream to gather job tree, then inspect_job_run for failed/suspicious jobs
Calculate (sections gathered / sections requested) × 100. All of {{sections}} must have data in the analysis
Count categories analyzed: (1) breaking issues, (2) performance issues, (3) design anti-patterns, (4) resource waste. Each category must have explicit findings or 'none detected' statement

Calculate (issues with fully traced root cause / total issues detected) × 100. A 'fully traced root cause' MUST include: (1) originating job ID, (2) specific configuration that caused failure (blueprint field, enabledTools entry, env var, dispatch param), (3) explanation of WHY that configuration led to failure, (4) dispatch chain showing how error propagated, (5) telemetry evidence. NOT acceptable: 'job failed because X happened' - must explain what CONFIGURATION caused X.
Calculate (issues with severity / total issues) × 100. Severity levels: CRITICAL (blocking), WARNING (degraded), INFO (optimization). Each must have justification
Score 0-100: job coverage (25), section coverage (15), all categories checked (20), root causes traced (20), severities assigned (10), evidence cited (10). Range prevents over-analysis

For each issue, root cause MUST trace to specific configuration: blueprint field, enabledTools entry, environment variable, template setting, or dispatch parameter. Not just 'what failed' but 'what configuration caused it to fail'. Data comes from investigation children.

For EACH detected issue, dispatch a 'Root Cause Investigator' child job to trace the issue to its configuration source. Input: issue description, affected job IDs. Output: configuration-level explanation (blueprint, enabledTools, env vars, template). Count dispatch_new_job calls for root cause investigations.

invariants.condition: Wait for investigation children to complete and synthesize their findings. You will be RE-INVOKED when children complete - context.hierarchy.children will contain their results.
invariants.assessment: On first invocation: triage issues and dispatch investigators. On re-invocation (children present): synthesize child findings into final analysis with configuration-level root causes.
Count recommendations for each CRITICAL issue. Each critical issue must have at least one actionable fix proposal
Calculate (recommendations with what/where/how / total recommendations) × 100. Actionable = specifies what to change, where to change it, and how to verify

invariants.condition: You create a structured analysis artifact using create_artifact with topic 'WORKSTREAM_ANALYSIS' containing all required sections
invariants.assessment: Verify artifact exists with: metadata, executiveSummary, findings (with symptom, rootCause, recommendation for each), actionItems
Count action items in output divided by workstreams analyzed. Each workstream must produce at least one prioritized action item
